chunks.text:
The CLI ships in the opendetail package. Prefer npx, bunx, or pnpm dlx so you always run the version pinned by your project.

The opendetail package exposes build (index your docs), setup (scaffold config and route), and doctor (verify the setup).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Install and first run

If that prints a version, the executable resolves correctly from your package manager.

Running the CLI without a subcommand prints supported commands and example invocations (same sections below).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Typical flows

Greenfield app: opendetail setup to write opendetail.toml, scaffold the default route (self-hosted), and optionally run an initial index build.Docs changed: opendetail build to refresh .opendetail/index.json.Something looks wrong: opendetail doctor to verify config, index, route, and environment expectations.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Command overview

| Command | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail build | Write .opendetail/index.json from your config and content globs. |
| opendetail setup | Create opendetail.toml, optional route, optional first build. |
| opendetail doctor | Check files and env for the selected integration mode. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
One-liner usage

`opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail setup [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail doctor [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Suggested order

setup once per repo (or when you change integration mode).build whenever docs or media globs change.doctor when something fails or after upgrading packages.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
build

opendetail build is the index build step: it resolves paths from your config, walks the include globs, and writes .opendetail/index.json (or --output).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --config | Path to opendetail.toml (default: ./opendetail.toml). |
| --output | Where to write the index JSON (default: .opendetail/index.json relative to cwd). |
| --cwd | Working directory for resolving paths. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
setup

opendetail setup writes the standard project shape for either self-hosted (local Next route + OpenAI) or hosted (client talks to your deployed endpoint).
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail setup [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
What it can write

opendetail.tomlIn self-hosted mode, a route at src/app/api/opendetail/route.ts by default (override with --route).opendetail/index.json unless you pass --skip-build
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Important flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --integration | self-hosted or hosted. |
| --config | Custom config path. |
| --route | Custom route file path (self-hosted). |
| --base-path | Public source URL base (default /docs). |
| --include | Docs glob (default matches typical content/**/*.{md,mdx}). |
| --media-include | Media glob when media is enabled. |
| --with-media | Include [media] in config. |
| --skip-build | Skip the initial index build. |
| --force | Overwrite generated files. |
| --interactive / --no-interactive | Control the setup wizard when stdin is a TTY. |
| --cwd | Run against another working directory. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
doctor

opendetail doctor runs a checklist and prints ✓ or ✗ for each item. It exits with an error if anything required is missing.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail doctor [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Self-hosted expectations

Config: opendetail.toml (or --config) exists.Index: .opendetail/index.json (or configured output) exists.Route: the API route file exists at the configured path.Env: OPENAI_API_KEY is set where the route runs.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Hosted expectations

Config and index as above.Env: OPENDETAIL_ENDPOINT points at your hosted OpenDetail HTTP API.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: The opendetail package is the engine: it reads opendetail.toml, builds .opendetail/index.json, and exposes createOpenDetail for answering from that index. The CLI (build, setup, doctor) lives in the same package — see CLI.
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
What ships in the package

| Area | Role |
| --- | --- |
| Build | Parse matched Markdown/MDX, chunk by headings, write the index (CLI or buildOpenDetailIndex). |
| Runtime | Load the index, lexical search, Responses API, stream NDJSON — Create an assistant. |
| CLI | Setup, index builds, doctor — CLI. |
| Config | opendetail.toml — Fields. |
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
When to use it directly

Use opendetail without Next or React for Node integrations (custom server, scripts, tests). For Next.js App Router, use opendetail-next; for browser UI, use opendetail-react and UI integration.
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Minimal config

OpenDetail reads opendetail.toml at the project root.

`version = 1

[[content]]
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/docs"
`
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Fields

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| version | yes | Must be 1 |
| [[content]] | yes | One or more tables: each has include, exclude, and public_path for a Markdown/MDX root |
| media | no | Rules for mapping local media references to public URLs |
| fetch | no | Optional OpenAI Responses API tools: [fetch.file_search] and/or [fetch.web_search] |
| knowledge | no | Path to optional .opendetail/knowledge.toml sidecar with [[asset]] titles/summaries for images |
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Content roots

You can list multiple [[content]] blocks when docs, marketing, or other MDX trees live under different folders and are served at different URL prefixes. Each row is one indexed tree. Set public_path to the public URL prefix for that tree (where those pages appear in the browser), for example /docs for documentation and / for site-root marketing. OpenDetail joins each file’s route segments after that prefix to build citation URLs (and, beyond the first root, tags chunks as sourceKind: "page" for UI styling).
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Media

Use [media] when docs reference local images such as ./hero.png.

`[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/content-media"
`

When media is configured:

http:// and https:// URLs are returned as-isroot-relative /... URLs are returned as-islocal relative paths are mapped only if they match [media].includeunsupported absolute schemes and protocol-relative URLs are ignored

If a doc references a local image that does not match the configured media globs, the build fails with a clear error.
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Fetch (OpenAI remote tools)

Optional Responses API tools — configure as [fetch.file_search] and/or [fetch.web_search] (native remote retrieval; no same-origin HTML scraping).

`[fetch.file_search]
vector_store_ids = ["vs_123"]
max_num_results = 8

[fetch.web_search]
allowed_domains = ["opendetail.dev", "platform.openai.com"]
search_context_size = "low"
`

file_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| vector_store_ids | yes | OpenAI vector store IDs |
| max_num_results | no | Limit from 1 to 50 |

web_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| allowed_domains | no | Restrict search to listed domains |
| search_context_size | no | low, medium, or high |

Remote citations appear in sources with kind: "remote".
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Knowledge sidecar

Optional knowledge = ".opendetail/knowledge.toml" with entries such as:

`[[asset]]
url = "/content-media/diagram.png"
title = "Pipeline overview"
summary = "Use when explaining the build step."
`
chunks.title: Core package
Core package

chunks.text:
Assistant instructions

The OpenAI Responses API separates instructions (how the model behaves)
from input (sources + question). OpenDetail merges harness and project
text into a single instructions string:

Harness (built-in): informational assistant behavior, grounding, citations, tools.Project (your files below): tone, priorities, product vocabulary — keep it short.

Loaded in order (project append):

assistantInstructions optionassistantInstructionsPath option.opendetail/OPENDETAIL.mdOPENDETAIL.md at project root

If none exist, only built-in harness instructions apply.
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Remote retrieval

Configure via fetch in createOpenDetail options or [fetch] in opendetail.toml (fetch.file_search, fetch.web_search). Runtime options override the artifact when provided.
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Defaults

| Setting | Default |
| --- | --- |
| model | gpt-5.4-mini |
| reasoningEffort | none |
| verbosity | low |
| store | false |
| promptCacheRetention | in-memory |
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Important options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| cwd | Working directory for config and instruction files |
| indexPath | Custom path to the built index |
| indexData | Preloaded index artifact |
| client | Reuse an existing OpenAI client |
| assistantInstructions | Inline project guidance |
| assistantInstructionsPath | Custom instructions path |
| promptCacheKey | Explicit cache key override |
| fetch | Override fetch from the index (OpenAI tools) |
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
Fallback behavior

If lexical retrieval matches no indexed chunks, OpenDetail sets fallback: true. The model may still answer using configured fetch tools; the flag means “no index hit,” not “no answer.” If the model returns no usable text:

`That isn't covered in the available sources yet.`
chunks.title: Core package

chunks.text:
OpenDetail splits systems (full UI identity: structure, semantics, layout) from themes (palette layers on top of one system). Primitives are the documented React components that implement the system. The default OpenDetail system and Midnight / Signal themes ship with opendetail-react styles.

Headless API (same for every system): React — useOpenDetail, createOpenDetailClient, primitives, and hooks.
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
Systems

A system is a full UI identity for the assistant: semantic class contract, layout and density, motion, and component anatomy (thread, composer, messages, sources). The default OpenDetail system ships in opendetail-react under src/styles/systems/opendetail/ (aggregated by opendetail-base.css).

Add a new system when you need different chrome or structure—not just different colors—while keeping the same headless transport (useOpenDetail, createOpenDetailClient) and message shapes.
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
Systems vs themes

Themes are palette layers inside one system: same layout and components, different tokens. See Themes.Systems stay isolated when a product line needs different chrome but the same streaming protocol.In the repo: new colors → themes/; new chrome → systems/.
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
Import order (system CSS)

Import the base system stylesheet (opendetail-base.css or systems/opendetail/index.css).Optionally layer a theme after base (opendetail-midnight.css, opendetail-signal.css, or a custom palette file).

Root elements use data-od-system="opendetail" where layout marks the design system for theming.
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
Themes

A theme is a palette (and optional minor token tweaks) inside one system: background, foreground, accent, borders. Layout and React primitives stay the same; only paint changes.

Themes are palette layers on top of opendetail-base.css: same layout classes and components; only tokens change. Import base first, then one theme file.
chunks.title: Styling
Styling
Styling

chunks.text:
Adding your own

Custom themes belong next to the base import: override semantic CSS variables scoped to data-od-system="opendetail" (or your system) without forking React primitives.
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
Primitives

Primitives are the documented React exports that implement the OpenDetail system: layout regions, the thread and composer, messages, sources, and status—styled by opendetail-base.css and optional themes. They are not the headless streaming layer; that lives in useOpenDetail and createOpenDetailClient (useOpenDetail, createOpenDetailClient, full reference React).
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
What ships

Layout — ConversationLayout (thread + composer regions); AssistantSidebar and AssistantModal compose it for a full column or modal assistant.Conversation UI — Thread, Composer, UserMessage, AssistantMessage, AssistantSources, suggestions, AssistantStatus, and source link helpers.Styles — Structural tokens and component classes from the system stylesheet; palette from themes.

Recipes (AssistantSidebar, AssistantModal) bundle primitives for a drop-in experience; you can also compose primitives inside your own shell.

See React for primitive exports and composition; UI integration shows how to wire your own shell.
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
See also

React API · UI integration · style README
chunks.title: Styling

chunks.text:
opendetail-fumadocs is optional glue for apps that already serve docs under predictable URLs (often Fumadocs-style /docs/... routes). It does not add a different assistant runtime: it supplies resolveSourceTarget helpers and a thin FumadocsAssistant wrapper around AssistantSidebar from opendetail-react, wired with renderNextSourceLink from opendetail-next.

For when to use this vs hand-wiring, see Packages & composition.
chunks.title: Fumadocs

chunks.text:
API

resolveFumadocsSourceTarget — Given knownPageUrls and a source (kind: "local" or "remote"), returns { external, href } or null for unknown local paths.createFumadocsSourceTargetResolver(knownPageUrls) — Returns a ResolveAssistantSourceTarget you pass to AssistantSidebar as resolveSourceTarget.getFumadocsPageUrls(source) — Maps a Fumadocs-style source with getPages() to a flat list of URLs for the allowlist.
chunks.title: Fumadocs
Fumadocs
Fumadocs

chunks.text: Use createOpenDetailClient when you do not want the useOpenDetail hook: vanilla JS, a custom store, or non-React surfaces. It speaks the same POST body and application/x-ndjson stream as the hook.
chunks.title: createOpenDetailClient
createOpenDetailClient

chunks.text:
Status and events

Request status follows idle → pending → streaming → idle or error. Stream events include meta, sources, images, delta, done, error. Call stop() on the client to abort the active request.

Full API: client options, headers, credentials, and error handling — React.
chunks.title: createOpenDetailClient

chunks.text: useOpenDetail is the primary browser integration for OpenDetail: it manages messages, question, submit, stop, clearThread, request status, and error fields, while streaming assistant text, sources, and images from your POST /api/opendetail (or equivalent) route.
chunks.title: useOpenDetail
useOpenDetail

chunks.text:
Common options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Shortcut for transport.endpoint (default /api/opendetail). |
| transport.headers | Static or function for per-request headers. |
| persistence.key / persistence.storage | Restore thread + draft after refresh (local or session). |

Wire UI with Composer (requestState={status}), AssistantSidebar (connection={{ endpoint, persistence, … }}), or your own layout.

Full API: tables for every option, persistence behavior, message shapes, and stream lifecycle — React.
chunks.title: useOpenDetail

chunks.text: OpenDetail turns the Markdown and MDX you already ship into grounded answers inside your product: same content your team edits, retrieved with citations instead of vibes. It is intentionally small: a build step, a local index file, a narrow HTTP contract, and thin adapters — not a second CMS and not a generic chat platform.
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
How it fits together

| Stage | What happens |
| --- | --- |
| Build | Matched docs become .opendetail/index.json. |
| Request | Your route or client loads the index, runs lexical search, calls the Responses API with that context. |
| UI | Streamed NDJSON (text, sources, images) — you render with your components or opendetail-react. |

Docs stay human-readable on the web; machine-readable routes (for example /llms.mdx/docs) remain available for tooling.
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Who it is for

| Audience | Why |
| --- | --- |
| Product & devrel | In-app help without a separate knowledge base. |
| Engineering | Testable pipeline: artifact in git, predictable API, clear errors. |
| Agents | Stable URLs and structured retrieval from the same index. |
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Packages (at a glance)

| Package | Role |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail | Build, core runtime, CLI. |
| opendetail-next | Next.js App Router route, NDJSON streaming, renderNextSourceLink. |
| opendetail-react | useOpenDetail, AssistantSidebar (connection for streaming), streaming client. |
| opendetail-fumadocs | Optional: known-page resolver + FumadocsAssistant. |
| trifold | Optional: parallel columns for small viewports. |

Layering and naming details: Packages & composition.

Styling: systems, themes, and primitives are covered in Styling. Component APIs (exports and hooks) are documented in React.
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Where to go next

CLI: CLI · Integrations: Next.js · React · Fumadocs
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text: opendetail-next wraps the core runtime for self-hosted deployments on the Next.js App Router: a POST handler that validates the request body, streams NDJSON, and optional renderNextSourceLink for assistant citations in MDX.
chunks.title: Next.js
Next.js

chunks.text:
Request and response

The handler accepts POST with JSON { "question": string }. Malformed bodies return a JSON error.

Successful responses use application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8. Event order and semantics match Get started (meta, sources, images, delta, done, error). Initialization failures before streaming return JSON instead of NDJSON.
chunks.title: Next.js

chunks.text:
Source links in MDX

For Fumadocs-style docs, pass renderNextSourceLink from opendetail-next into your assistant UI so local /docs/... citations use Next Link and external URLs stay safe:

`import { renderNextSourceLink } from "opendetail-next";
`

Use with resolveSourceTarget from opendetail-fumadocs when you need known-page URL allowlists, or see Packages & composition for how the stack fits together.
chunks.title: Next.js

chunks.text: OpenDetail is intentionally layered: core runtime and build, then framework adapters, then UI. You pick the layers your app needs; they compose in one codebase.
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text:
Layers (bottom to top)

opendetail — CLI, config, opendetail build, .opendetail/index.json, and the core runtime that loads the index and talks to the model. Start here for any integration.opendetail-next — Next.js App Router: createNextRoute / createNextRouteHandler, NDJSON streaming, and renderNextSourceLink so assistant citations can render as next/link where appropriate.opendetail-react — Browser UI: useOpenDetail, createOpenDetailClient, AssistantSidebar (with optional connection for the streaming client), and related assistant UI. Framework-agnostic (works with Vite, etc.) as long as you point the client at your HTTP endpoint.opendetail-fumadocs (optional) — A small preset on top of opendetail-react + opendetail-next: createFumadocsSourceTargetResolver, FumadocsAssistant, and helpers for known doc URLs. The name reflects common Fumadocs-style /docs/... routes; the code does not import Fumadocs packages — it is an allowlisted pathname resolver you can use for any Next docs site that lists its routes.
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text: trifold (optional, separate package) — Parallel columns for small viewports (leading · center · trailing geometry). The OpenDetail web demo uses it inside renderMobileShell; it is not required for retrieval or streaming.
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text:
When to use which package

| Package | Use when |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail | You need the build, CLI, runtime, or self-hosted index file. |
| opendetail-next | You deploy on Next.js App Router and want the official route handler + renderNextSourceLink. |
| opendetail-react | You build the in-browser assistant (hook, shell, streaming client). |
| opendetail-fumadocs | You want a known-page allowlist for citation URLs and/or the FumadocsAssistant wrapper instead of wiring resolveSourceTarget by hand. Skip it if you implement your own resolver (e.g. internal tools, multiple origins). |
| trifold | You want the parallel-column mobile shell UX (Trifold / ParallelTrack); purely presentational. |
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text:
How they compound

Typical Next.js + self-hosted app:

opendetail (build + runtime) +opendetail-next (route + renderNextSourceLink) +opendetail-react (useOpenDetail + AssistantSidebar).

Optional additions:

opendetail-fumadocs — same stack, plus createFumadocsSourceTargetResolver([...]) passed into the shell (or use FumadocsAssistant, which bundles resolver + link renderer + shell — see opendetail-fumadocs).trifold — only the mobile shell layout; compose via renderMobileShell on the assistant shell, not inside core packages.

There is one assistant pipeline (question → NDJSON → UI). FumadocsAssistant is not a second product — it is AssistantSidebar with connection, Next-aware source links, and a preset resolver.
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text:
Naming in this monorepo

| Name | Role |
| --- | --- |
| AssistantSidebar | opendetail-react — slots for navigation, main content, and the assistant column. |
| FumadocsAssistant | opendetail-fumadocs/assistant — convenience wrapper: AssistantSidebar + renderNextSourceLink + allowlist resolver. |
| Sidebar | apps/web only — marketing site nav (not published as a package). |

If you outgrow the allowlist (e.g. dynamic internal URLs), keep opendetail-react + opendetail-next and supply your own resolveSourceTarget; you do not need opendetail-fumadocs.
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text:
See also

Get started — install, integration modes, stream shape, grounding.Core package — opendetail.toml and createOpenDetail.UI integration — custom UI; React — primitives and hooks.Next.js — route handler and renderNextSourceLink.React — useOpenDetail and client API.Fumadocs — resolver API and FumadocsAssistant.
chunks.title: Packages & composition

chunks.text: Follow this page in order once; after that, rebuild whenever docs change.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Install

Add the packages you need for a Next.js app with the default route adapter:

Use the copy control to cycle package managers (npm i, Yarn, pnpm, or Bun) with the same dependency list. Check that the CLI is available without a global install:
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Scaffold

Generate opendetail.toml, the default API route, and the first index (unless you skip the build):

What that can do:

write opendetail.tomlscaffold src/app/api/opendetail/route.ts (self-hosted default)run an initial build to produce .opendetail/index.json

Add OPENAI_API_KEY to the environment your route runs in before you call the API.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Default config shape

You should see something like:

`version = 1

[[content]]
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/docs"

[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
public_path = "/content-media"
`

Tweak [[content]] globs and public_path so each root matches where those files are served on the site. Add more [[content]] rows when you index multiple trees (for example /docs and /). Full field list is in Core package.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Rebuild when content changes

Any time Markdown, MDX, or matched media changes:

Run that in CI before deploy, or as a postbuild step, so production always matches the docs you shipped.

Diagnose when something looks wrong:

CLI details: CLI.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text: Integration modes
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Self-hosted (default)

Self-hosted means: your Next.js route, your Node runtime, your API key, your rebuilt index on deploy.

The App Router integration lives in opendetail-next: createNextRoute, createNextRouteHandler, and renderNextSourceLink. See opendetail-next for exports, options, and requirements. Package layering: Packages & composition.

| Requirement | Notes |
| --- | --- |
| Node.js | 18+ |
| Runtime | Node.js for the default adapter (not Edge) |
| Secrets | OPENAI_API_KEY where the handler runs |
| Artifact | .opendetail/index.json after build |

Request body — intentionally tiny:

`{ "question": "How do I configure public_path?" }`

Malformed bodies get a JSON error instead of a broken stream.

Response — application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8, events in this order:

| Event | Role |
| --- | --- |
| meta | Model metadata |
| sources | Matched chunks (and remote citations when configured) |
| images | Grounded image references from matched docs |
| delta | Streamed answer text |
| done | Final text |
| error | Safe public error payload |
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text: If initialization fails before streaming starts, you get JSON instead of NDJSON. In production, repeated init failures are cached so you do not pay the same failure cost on every request.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Hosted-shaped (scaffold only)

Hosted mode in the CLI does not generate a route. It still writes config and builds .opendetail/index.json, but your app is expected to call a remote endpoint you configure — the OpenDetail-managed cloud backend is not shipped yet.

Point the client at your future endpoint with environment variables:

| Variable | Typical use |
| --- | --- |
| OPENDETAIL_ENDPOINT | Server-side URL |
| OPENDETAIL_AUTH_TOKEN | Optional bearer token |
| NEXT_PUBLIC_OPENDETAIL_* | Browser examples |

Request and stream events stay the same as self-hosted so you do not need a breaking migration later.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Grounding (short)

Build time — Matched files are parsed, split into heading-based chunks, and written to .opendetail/index.json. No required vector database in the default path.Request time — The runtime loads the index, runs lexical search, sends the best chunks to the Responses API, and optionally attaches remote tools from config.Instructions — The model is told to answer from those sources, cite factual statements, avoid inventing coverage, and admit when the topic is not in the docs.

If nothing matches well, you still get a response, but it may be marked as fallback; if the model produces no usable text, users see a clear “not documented yet” style message — see fallback.
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
Ask the first question

With the default route, POST JSON:

`{ "question": "How do I install this?" }`

Use createOpenDetailClient or useOpenDetail from opendetail-react if you are building UI — see React and UI integration.
chunks.title: Get started

images.title: Thread shell — composer anchored, reading column
Streaming response — sources and media alongside partial text

chunks.text:
Reference layouts (this site)

These SVGs show patterns you might mirror: a narrow reading column with a fixed composer, and streaming layout with sources and images.

Thread shell reference

Streaming response reference
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text:
FAQ

| Topic | Answer |
| --- | --- |
| Chat UI? | OpenDetail is the engine and contracts; you bring the UI — or compose from opendetail-react (UI integration). |
| Vector DB? | Not required for the default local index path. |
| MDX with components? | Text and headings matter for retrieval; exotic runtime components are not the retrieval unit. |
| Hosted backend? | Scaffolded client shape exists; managed backend is not shipped in this phase. |
chunks.title: Get started

chunks.text: Package: opendetail-react. For a Next.js route that serves the same NDJSON stream, see opendetail-next. For AssistantSidebar with a connection prop, optional opendetail-fumadocs, and how packages compound, see Packages & composition. Styling (systems, themes, primitives): Styling. Custom UI: UI integration. Hook docs: useOpenDetail, createOpenDetailClient (full API: React).
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
React hook

opendetail-react exports useOpenDetail for browser state and streaming UI.
chunks.title: React
React

chunks.text:
Options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Shortcut for transport.endpoint |
| transport.endpoint | Request URL, defaults to /api/opendetail |
| transport.headers | Static headers or a function returning headers |
| transport.credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| transport.fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
| persistence.key | Storage key for thread state |
| persistence.storage | local or session, default local |
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Returned state

messages, question, setQuestion, submit, stop, clearThread, status, and error fields such as error, errorCode, errorRetryable, errorStatus.

Assistant messages track streamed text, sources, images, model, duration label, error metadata, and status: pending | streaming | complete | error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Persistence

When enabled, thread and input are restored; interrupted streams are marked honestly after refresh.
chunks.title: React
React

chunks.text:
Client options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Request URL, default /api/opendetail |
| headers | Static or function |
| credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Request

`{ "question": "..." }`

Headers: accept: application/x-ndjson, application/json, content-type: application/json.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Status values

idle → pending → streaming → idle or error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Stream event types

meta, sources, images, delta, done, error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Stop behavior

stop() aborts the active request. Calling submit() while a request is active stops the previous one first.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Use this page when you are building custom UI—your layout, your components, your styling. For systems, themes, and primitive styling, see Styling; for exports and the streaming hook, see React.

Package APIs: opendetail-react, opendetail-next.
chunks.title: UI integration

chunks.text:
Building blocks

| Approach | Primary APIs |
| --- | --- |
| Hook + your layout | useOpenDetail |
| HTTP client, no hook | createOpenDetailClient |
| Shell preset | AssistantSidebar with connection — React |
| Citations as Next links | renderNextSourceLink |
| Allowlisted doc URLs | opendetail-fumadocs (optional) |

NDJSON route setup and Node requirements: opendetail-next. Request shape and stream order: Get started. Hook, client, and events: React.
chunks.title: UI integration

chunks.text:
Package exports

opendetail-react documents useOpenDetail, createOpenDetailClient, AssistantMessage, AssistantSources, AssistantSidebar, and related exports. Take the pieces you need; the full shell is optional.

chunks.title: Changelog

headings: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.text: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.title: Changelog

headings: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.text:
Build output

The CLI writes a single machine-readable index.json under .opendetail, so apps can load chunks for lexical search without a separate database. Paths stay stable across builds when content hashes do not change.
chunks.title: Changelog

headings: v0.5.0 — Docs index and streaming UX
chunks.text:
Assistant

The sidebar assistant accepts streamed NDJSON (text, sources, images) so the UI can render incrementally. Known doc URLs are passed through for safe source links in Fumadocs-style layouts.

Changes: Stricter validation for remote tool definitions in hosted mode; clearer errors when the index file is missing or out of date; minor CSS fixes for the input surface on small viewports.
chunks.title: Changelog

chunks.text:
v0.4.0 — CLI quickstart and configuration

opendetail setup can scaffold self-hosted and hosted-shaped configs, with flags for base path, include globs, and optional media. opendetail doctor checks binary, config, and route wiring before you ship.

Changes: Configuration fields documented; improved progress output during opendetail build.
chunks.title: Changelog

chunks.text: OpenDetail — grounded answers from your own Markdown and MDX. Built for product teams who already ship docs and for developers who want a small, inspectable pipeline (local index file, narrow API, streaming events you can actually render).
chunks.title: OpenDetail

chunks.text:
In one sentence

Index docs at build time, answer from that index at request time, cite sources instead of guessing — same content humans read on the web, wired for your app and for agents that consume machine-readable routes.
chunks.title: OpenDetail

chunks.text:
Try the assistant here

Press ⌘ / Ctrl + J to open the sidebar and ask questions against this site's docs index.
chunks.title: OpenDetail
